overview.subtitle: What is the Polytechnic Diploma Programme?
paragraphs: The Polytechnic Diploma in Engineering is the fastest and most practical route to enter the core technical sector. Unlike traditional 11th and 12th grades which focus purely on theory, this program drops students directly into the world of engineering mechanics, circuits, drafting, and programming.

The pedagogy is heavily skewed towards practical learning—nearly 70% of the course is taught inside advanced workshops, electrical labs, and computer centers. Students learn how to operate heavy machinery, draft blueprints using AutoCAD, and build functional electrical circuits, making them instantly valuable to manufacturing and IT companies.

scope.body: A Polytechnic Diploma offers immense career security and quick employment. Graduates are hired immediately by manufacturing plants, construction companies, and IT firms as Junior Engineers (JE), Draftsmen, Site Supervisors, and Technical Support Executives. Moreover, it is the primary qualification to appear for highly secure government exams like RRB JE (Railways) and SSC JE. Alternatively, students can use this diploma to get direct 'Lateral Entry' into the 2nd year of a B.Tech degree, bypassing the 1st year entirely.
scope.subtitle: Expanding opportunities in manufacturing, IT, and government sectors

curriculum.subtitle: A hands-on curriculum designed for industrial execution
curriculum.introNote: The first year builds a common foundation in basic sciences and engineering drawing, while the next two years focus entirely on the chosen core branch.
curriculum.outroNote: A mandatory Industrial Training phase (Summer Internship) is required to ensure real-world factory or IT exposure.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: The 3-year diploma is divided into 6 semesters. The 1st year acts as a bridge, teaching applied sciences and the basics of Engineering Graphics. From the 2nd year (3rd Semester), students dive deep into their chosen specialization (Mechanical, Civil, Electrical, or CS), spending maximum hours in specialized labs. The final year focuses on advanced machinery/code and a mandatory minor project.

accordionSections.title: Year-1: Common Engineering Foundation
accordionSections.content: Applied Mathematics I & II, Applied Physics I & II, Applied Chemistry, Engineering Graphics/Drawing, Basic IT Skills, Manufacturing Processes (Workshop Practice).

accordionSections.title: Year-2: Core Branch Subjects
accordionSections.content: Introduction to branch-specific subjects (e.g., Fluid Mechanics for ME, Surveying for CE, Data Structures for CS). Heavy emphasis on respective departmental laboratories, AutoCAD, and basic design principles.

accordionSections.title: Year-3: Advanced Tech & Industrial Training
accordionSections.content: Advanced branch-specific subjects (e.g., CNC Machines, Power Systems, Advanced Java). Includes a 4-6 week Mandatory Industrial Internship, Entrepreneurship Development, and a Final Year Technical Project.

accordionSections.title: Available Branches (Specializations)
accordionSections.content: 1. Mechanical Engineering, 2. Civil Engineering, 3. Electrical Engineering, 4. Computer Science Engineering.

overview.subtitle: What is the B.Tech CSE Programme?
paragraphs: The B.Tech in Computer Science Engineering (CSE) is the flagship program of our university. It is explicitly designed to bridge the massive gap between traditional academic syllabi and the modern, high-speed requirements of Silicon Valley and global IT giants.

From Day 1, the pedagogy shifts from theoretical textbook learning to a hardcore, hands-on coding culture. Students are rigorously trained in Data Structures and Algorithms (DSA), Object-Oriented Programming (OOPs), Database Management, and scalable System Design. With 24/7 access to advanced computing labs and a thriving competitive programming environment, graduates emerge not just as coders, but as elite Software Architects.

scope.body: Computer Science is the backbone of the modern digital economy. A B.Tech CSE graduate is aggressively sought after by Product-Based Companies (Amazon, Microsoft, Google), leading IT Service providers (TCS, Infosys, Wipro), and hyper-growth tech startups. High-paying career roles include Software Development Engineer (SDE), Backend/Frontend Developer, Cloud Solutions Architect, and Database Administrator (DBA). Furthermore, it is the perfect launchpad for pursuing an MS in Computer Science abroad or launching an independent tech startup.
scope.subtitle: Expanding opportunities in the global software industry

curriculum.subtitle: A Silicon Valley-aligned curriculum for elite engineers
curriculum.introNote: The curriculum is systematically structured to build algorithmic logic in the early years and advanced software architecture in the latter years.
curriculum.outroNote: A mandatory 6-month Industrial Internship (or major capstone project) is required in the final semester to ensure absolute industry readiness.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: The 4-year B.Tech CSE program spans 8 semesters. Year 1 focuses on engineering mathematics, basic electricals, and an introduction to programming (C/C++). Year 2 dives into the holy trinity of CS: DSA, OOPS, and Databases. Year 3 covers advanced system-level subjects (OS, Networks) and web development. Year 4 is dedicated entirely to specialized electives, campus placements, and a 6-month corporate internship.

accordionSections.title: Year-1: Engineering Foundations & Logic (Sem 1 & 2)
accordionSections.content: Engineering Mathematics I & II, Engineering Physics, Basic Electrical & Electronics, Introduction to C Programming, Engineering Graphics, Communication Skills, Physics & Programming Labs.

accordionSections.content: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA), Object-Oriented Programming (Java/C++), Discrete Mathematics, Computer Organization & Architecture (COA), Database Management Systems (DBMS), Software Engineering, Advanced Coding Labs.

accordionSections.title: Year-3: System Design & Web Technologies (Sem 5 & 6)
accordionSections.content: Operating Systems (OS), Computer Networks, Theory of Computation (TOC), Compiler Design, Full Stack Web Development (HTML/CSS/JS/Node), AI Basics, Minor Project (Hackathon Build).

accordionSections.title: Year-4: Specialization & Industrial Training (Sem 7 & 8)
accordionSections.content: Cloud Computing, Cryptography & Network Security, Departmental Electives (IoT, Big Data, App Dev), Major Capstone Project, 6-Month Mandatory Industrial Internship.

overview.subtitle: What is the B.Tech AI & ML Programme?
paragraphs: The B.Tech in Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning (AI & ML) is a specialized computing degree tailored for the future of technology. While standard CSE focuses on general software development, this program is laser-focused on teaching computers how to learn from data, recognize patterns, and make autonomous decisions.

Students are trained rigorously in advanced Mathematics (Linear Algebra, Statistics), Python programming, and powerful AI frameworks like TensorFlow and PyTorch. Working on high-performance GPU clusters, students build live projects ranging from facial recognition systems and chatbots to predictive stock market algorithms.

scope.body: AI and Machine Learning is currently the most heavily funded and highest-paying sector in the global tech industry. Graduates are aggressively hired as Machine Learning Engineers, Data Scientists, AI Architects, and NLP Researchers. Top recruiters include tech giants like Google, Meta, Microsoft, OpenAI, and hundreds of funded AI startups. Furthermore, this degree sets a perfect foundation for pursuing advanced AI research or an MS in Data Science globally.

overview.subtitle: What is the B.Tech Cloud Computing Programme?
paragraphs: The B.Tech in Cloud Computing is a highly specialized engineering degree. Today, every major company—from Netflix to banks—runs on the Cloud. This program moves beyond traditional software development and focuses on how to deploy, host, scale, and secure those software applications on global server networks.

Students are rigorously trained in the core computer science subjects (DSA, OS, Networks) combined with deep specializations in AWS (Amazon Web Services), Microsoft Azure, virtualization, containerization (Docker/Kubernetes), and DevOps pipelines. Graduates emerge ready to manage the infrastructure of billion-dollar tech platforms.

scope.body: The transition to remote work and digital platforms has caused a massive global shortage of Cloud Computing professionals. Graduates are aggressively hired as Cloud Solutions Architects, DevOps Engineers, SysOps Administrators, and Cloud Security Experts. overview.subtitle: What is Software Product Engineering?
paragraphs: The B.Tech in Software Product Engineering is a unique, highly specialized degree. While traditional computer science focuses primarily on algorithms and raw coding, Product Engineering teaches you how to build a complete, marketable software application (like Uber, Zomato, or a SaaS tool) from scratch.

Students are trained in a 'Startup-like' environment. The curriculum covers Full-Stack Web and Mobile Development, UI/UX Design psychology, Cloud hosting, and Agile Product Management. By the time students graduate, they don't just have a resume; they have a portfolio of live, functioning software products with real users.

scope.body: The tech industry is shifting from hiring just 'programmers' to hiring 'Product Builders'. Graduates of this program are in massive demand at Product-Based Companies (Amazon, Flipkart, Swiggy, Zoho) and high-growth startups. Career roles include Full-Stack Engineer, Technical Product Manager (PM), UI/UX Developer, and Solutions Architect. Furthermore, this degree is the ultimate launchpad for students aiming to become Tech Founders (CTOs) and launch their own funded startups.

overview.subtitle: What is the M.Tech CSE Programme?
paragraphs: The Master of Technology (M.Tech) in Computer Science Engineering is an intensive, research-driven postgraduate program. While a B.Tech focuses on learning how to code and build applications, an M.Tech focuses on optimizing how those applications run at a massive scale. It shifts the focus from software development to software architecture and algorithmic research.

Students dive deep into High-Performance Computing (HPC), Advanced Data Structures, Artificial Intelligence, and Distributed Databases. The second year is almost entirely dedicated to an independent Master's Dissertation, where students solve complex computational problems, publish papers in IEEE/Springer journals, or file software patents.

scope.body: An M.Tech degree drastically elevates an engineer's technical authority. Graduates are heavily recruited by the R&D wings of tech giants (Microsoft Research, Google Brain, IBM) for specialized roles like Senior Software Architect, Data Scientist, and Machine Learning Researcher. Furthermore, an M.Tech is the mandatory qualification for joining engineering colleges as an Assistant Professor. It is also the ideal stepping stone for pursuing a Ph.D. in Computer Science at top global universities.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: The 2-year M.Tech CSE program operates on a 4-semester system. Semesters 1 and 2 involve intense coursework in advanced computer science theories and high-level programming labs. Semesters 3 and 4 are completely dedicated to independent research, where students work closely with faculty mentors to develop a novel algorithm or system, culminating in a thesis defense.

accordionSections.content: Mathematical Foundations of Computer Science, Advanced Data Structures and Algorithms, Advanced Database Systems, Machine Learning Basics, Advanced Computing Lab I. Total Credits: 24

accordionSections.content: High-Performance Computer Architecture, Advanced Operating Systems, Elective I (e.g., Big Data Analytics), Elective II (e.g., Cloud Security), Advanced Computing Lab II, Seminar. Total Credits: 24

accordionSections.title: Semester-3
accordionSections.content: Research Methodology and IPR, Elective III (e.g., Deep Learning), Elective IV (e.g., Quantum Computing), Dissertation Phase-I (Synopsis Submission & Literature Review). Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-4
accordionSections.content: Master's Dissertation Phase-II (Implementation, Research Paper Publication, and Final Defense), Viva-Voce. Total Credits: 22

overview.subtitle: What is the M.Tech Cyber Security Programme?
paragraphs: The M.Tech in Cyber Security is a highly specialized, elite technical degree. As the world moves entirely to digital infrastructure, cyber warfare and data breaches have become the biggest threats to corporations and governments. This program trains engineers not just to build software, but to break it, test it, and ultimately bulletproof it.

Students are immersed in a highly practical environment using advanced Cyber Security Simulation Labs (Cyber Ranges). The curriculum rigorously covers Penetration Testing, Malware Analysis, Advanced Cryptography, and Cloud Security. The final year involves an independent thesis or a live corporate project identifying and patching zero-day vulnerabilities.

scope.body: There is a massive global deficit of qualified Cyber Security professionals. Graduates are aggressively recruited as Security Operations Center (SOC) Analysts, Penetration Testers (Ethical Hackers), Cyber Security Architects, and Forensic Analysts. Top recruiters include global banks (JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs), Tech Giants (Microsoft, Amazon), leading IT service providers (TCS, Wipro), and Government Intelligence agencies (CBI, CERT-In). Furthermore, this degree is the ideal stepping stone for academic roles or pursuing a Ph.D. in Network Security.
scope.subtitle: Expanding opportunities in Global IT Security and Cyber Intelligence

curriculum.subtitle: An advanced curriculum for elite Cyber Defenders
curriculum.introNote: The curriculum dives immediately into advanced network architectures, transitioning into heavy offensive and defensive cyber tactics.
curriculum.outroNote: A mandatory Master's Dissertation (Research Thesis) or an intensive corporate security project must be defended to graduate.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: The 2-year M.Tech Cyber Security program spans 4 semesters. Semesters 1 and 2 involve intense coursework and practical labs focusing on cryptography, network defense, and ethical hacking. Semesters 3 and 4 are completely dedicated to independent research or corporate internships, where students work on securing live environments, culminating in a thesis defense.

accordionSections.title: Semester-1
accordionSections.content: Advanced Computer Networks, Cryptography and Data Security, Operating System Security (Linux/Windows), Principles of Information Security, Cyber Security Simulation Lab I. Total Credits: 24

accordionSections.title: Semester-2
accordionSections.content: Ethical Hacking and Penetration Testing, Digital Forensics and Incident Response, Cloud and Web Security, Elective I (e.g., Malware Analysis), Cyber Security Simulation Lab II, Seminar. Total Credits: 24

accordionSections.title: Semester-3
accordionSections.content: Research Methodology and IPR, Elective II (e.g., AI in Cyber Security), Elective III (e.g., Cyber Law & Policies), Dissertation Phase-I (Synopsis Submission & Vulnerability Assessment). Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-4
accordionSections.content: Master's Dissertation Phase-II (Implementation, Threat Mitigation, Research Paper Publication, and Final Defense), Viva-Voce. Total Credits: 22
